title: Dodge's Most Powerful Charger Sixpack Could Push 600 Horsepower
summary: While there's still no sign of a Hemi V-8, the Charger's six-cylinder variant is getting an upgrade that could substantially bump up the power.
whatHappening: Dodge is upgrading its Charger Sixpack six-cylinder engine to deliver around 600 horsepower, marking a significant power boost for the model. This enhancement comes as the automaker continues to develop its electric vehicle lineup while keeping performance-focused internal combustion options alive.
whoInvolved: Dodge, the American muscle car brand owned by Stellantis, is leading this engine development. The company has built its reputation on high-performance vehicles and is now pushing six-cylinder technology to compete with traditional V-8 expectations.
whyMatters: A 600-hp six-cylinder Charger could attract performance enthusiasts who want power without waiting for a traditional V-8 option that remains absent from Dodge's plans. This move signals Dodge's commitment to keeping combustion engines competitive as the industry shifts toward electrification, potentially extending the lifespan of its core performance franchise.
moreContext: Dodge has been repositioning itself as it transitions toward electrification, having already announced plans to phase out traditional engines. The Charger Sixpack was introduced as a six-cylinder alternative to fill the gap while customers awaited updates. The absence of a Hemi V-8 in the current generation has been a point of contention among purists, making this substantial six-cylinder upgrade a strategic move to maintain performance credibility during the brand's EV transition.

title: These Vehicles Are Dead for 2027
summary: Pour one out for greats like the BMW Z4, Lexus LC, Toyota GR Supra, and more.
whatHappening: Several beloved sports cars and specialty vehicles are being discontinued by major automakers, including the BMW Z4, Lexus LC, and Toyota GR Supra. These models won't be offered beyond 2027, marking the end of an era for performance-focused enthusiasts.
whoInvolved: BMW, Lexus (Toyota's luxury division), and Toyota are eliminating these nameplates from their lineups. These Japanese and German manufacturers have long positioned these vehicles as flagship performance and lifestyle products.
whyMatters: The discontinuations reflect automakers' shift toward electrification and profitability priorities, as traditional gas-powered sports cars face stricter emissions regulations and declining sales volumes. Enthusiasts are losing accessible, affordable performance options in an increasingly EV-dominated market.
moreContext: The automotive industry has aggressively pivoted toward electric vehicles to meet global emissions standards, making low-volume sports cars economically challenging to produce. The BMW Z4, Lexus LC, and Toyota GR Supra represented different market segments—roadster, luxury coupe, and lightweight sports car respectively—but all shared declining sales as consumers shifted toward SUVs and crossovers. Stricter upcoming emissions regulations in 2027 and beyond make it uneconomical to continue producing models with limited production runs. These discontinuations signal that traditional high-performance combustion vehicles are becoming luxury items reserved for ultra-premium brands, while mainstream manufacturers prioritize electrified alternatives.

title: Next-Generation BMW M3 Spotted Testing with Six-Cylinder Power
summary: BMW's iconic sports sedan will be offered with both electric and gas powertrains, and the six-cylinder M3 has now been spied testing at the Nürburgring.
whatHappening: BMW's next-generation M3 sports sedan is undergoing testing with a six-cylinder engine at the Nürburgring. The automaker is developing the iconic performance car with both traditional gas and electric powertrain options.
whoInvolved: BMW, the German luxury automaker known for its M performance division, is spearheading the M3's evolution. Car and Driver captured spy photos of the testing vehicle at Germany's famous racing circuit.
whyMatters: The M3's dual powertrain strategy reflects the automotive industry's shift toward electrification while maintaining performance credentials. This approach signals BMW's commitment to preserving its gas-engine heritage while meeting stricter emissions regulations and changing consumer preferences.
moreContext: The M3 has been a cornerstone of BMW's performance lineup since 1986, establishing the brand's reputation for high-performance sedans. Recent industry trends show luxury automakers increasingly offering hybrid and electric alternatives to traditionally gas-powered performance vehicles. BMW has already introduced electric versions in other segments, including the i7 and iX M60. The Nürburgring testing suggests BMW is validating both powertrains under demanding conditions to ensure comparable performance characteristics across the lineup.

title: Jensen Interceptor GTX Will Arrive as a Track-Only V-8 Bruiser
summary: Jensen International Automotive is reviving the iconic V-8 grand tourer, and the initially track-only Interceptor GTX will preview future road-going models.
whatHappening: Jensen International Automotive is reviving the legendary Interceptor nameplate with a new track-only V-8 model called the Interceptor GTX. The car will serve as a performance preview for future road-legal versions of the reborn grand tourer.
whoInvolved: Jensen International Automotive is the British automaker behind the revival of the iconic Interceptor brand, which was originally produced from 1966 to 1976. The company is working to resurrect one of the most celebrated V-8 grand tourers in automotive history.
whyMatters: The Interceptor GTX launch signals growing momentum in the retro-revival segment, where manufacturers are tapping nostalgia and performance enthusiasts' appetite for classic nameplates. Success with the track-focused model could validate demand for a road-going version, potentially establishing Jensen as a niche player competing against other bespoke automakers.
moreContext: The original Jensen Interceptor was a British grand tourer that gained cult status for its combination of style, performance, and luxury. Jensen ceased operations in 1976, leaving a 40+ year gap before the brand's revival. The track-only strategy mirrors industry trends seen with other heritage brands testing new models in motorsport before consumer launch. This approach allows Jensen to refine performance characteristics and generate buzz without the regulatory complexity of immediate road certification.

title: 2026 Lamborghini Temerario
summary: Review, Pricing, and Specs
whatHappening: Lamborghini is unveiling the 2026 Temerario, a new supercar model that marks a significant addition to the Italian automaker's lineup. The vehicle comes with detailed specifications, pricing information, and comprehensive reviews from automotive experts.
whoInvolved: Lamborghini, the Italian luxury supercar manufacturer owned by Audi (part of Volkswagen Group), is behind this release. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is providing the official review and specifications.
whyMatters: The Temerario's debut signals Lamborghini's continued evolution in the ultra-premium supercar market as consumer preferences shift toward new performance standards and potential electrification. The pricing and specs will influence how competitors position their high-end offerings and set expectations for the next generation of exotic vehicles.
moreContext: Lamborghini has been refreshing its model lineup over the past few years, transitioning from purely internal combustion engines toward hybrid and electric powertrains. The automaker previously discontinued iconic models like the Aventador to make room for new designs that balance performance with evolving emissions standards. The Temerario name continues Lamborghini's tradition of naming cars after legendary bulls, maintaining brand heritage while signaling a modern direction. This release reflects the broader automotive industry trend of luxury makers launching new platforms to compete in an increasingly regulated and technology-driven market.

title: Honda Kills Off the Electric Prologue SUV After Just Three Years
summary: Based on a Chevy Blazer EV, the Prologue will not make it to the 2027 model year after sales dropped sharply in 2026.
whatHappening: Honda is discontinuing the electric Prologue SUV after just three model years, ending production before the 2027 model year arrives. The decision comes as sales for the vehicle plummeted in 2026.
whoInvolved: Honda, the Japanese automaker, developed the Prologue as part of its EV expansion strategy. The vehicle was based on General Motors' Chevrolet Blazer EV platform, representing a partnership between the two automotive giants.
whyMatters: The Prologue's early death signals challenges in Honda's electrification strategy and raises questions about the viability of platform-sharing agreements with GM. This reflects broader struggles in the EV market where consumer demand has softened and competition remains fierce, particularly in the crowded electric SUV segment.
moreContext: The Prologue launched for the 2024 model year as Honda's answer to the growing EV SUV market, leveraging GM's Ultium platform to accelerate development. Built on the same architecture as the Chevy Blazer EV and Equinox EV, the vehicle was positioned as a premium electric option. However, it faced stiff competition from established players and newer EV brands, and pricing pressures in the EV market intensified throughout 2025-2026. The sharp sales decline in 2026 apparently convinced Honda that continuing the model was no longer economically viable.

title: 'Conversational' AI Software Is Coming to Some Hondas and Acuras
summary: Nine Honda and three Acura models will get updated software featuring Google Gemini, an AI assistant that listens and talks to drivers in a conversational tone.
whatHappening: Honda and Acura are rolling out Google Gemini AI assistant software to 12 vehicle models combined. The conversational AI will let drivers interact with their cars through natural dialogue rather than traditional voice commands or touchscreen menus.
whoInvolved: Honda and Acura (luxury division of Honda) are partnering with Google to integrate Gemini, Google's latest AI assistant. Nine Honda models and three Acura models will receive the update, representing a significant deployment of advanced AI in mainstream vehicles.
whyMatters: This represents the automotive industry's shift toward conversational interfaces that could improve driver safety and convenience by reducing the need to take eyes off the road or hands off the wheel. It also signals how major automakers are betting on generative AI to differentiate their in-car experience as a competitive advantage in an increasingly software-driven market.
moreContext: Automakers have been gradually integrating voice assistants for years, but conversational AI powered by large language models like Gemini represents a generational leap in capability and natural interaction. Google has been aggressively positioning Gemini across consumer products as a differentiator from rivals like ChatGPT and Claude. This partnership follows the broader automotive trend of enhanced infotainment systems becoming key selling points, with companies like Tesla and legacy automakers investing heavily in software and AI capabilities to compete in the EV-dominated future.

title: This Crazy V-12-Powered Aston Martin SUV Is Ready for Battle
summary: The Dreadnought will exist solely in the virtual world of Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, and it looks like an absolute beast.
whatHappening: Aston Martin has designed a V-12-powered SUV called the Dreadnought that's making its debut as a virtual vehicle in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4. The beast-like SUV exists exclusively in the video game's virtual world.
whoInvolved: Aston Martin, the legendary British luxury automaker known for high-performance sports cars and James Bond vehicles, partnered with Activision's Call of Duty franchise. The collaboration brings the iconic car brand into the gaming space.
whyMatters: This partnership represents a growing trend of luxury brands leveraging gaming platforms to reach younger, digital-native audiences and build brand engagement beyond traditional automotive channels. For gamers, it adds exclusive branded content that enhances the Call of Duty experience with premium automotive design.
moreContext: Luxury and performance brands increasingly use video games as marketing channels, recognizing that gaming audiences represent a massive and valuable demographic. Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 continues the franchise's tradition of featuring premium branded vehicles and equipment. Aston Martin has a storied history of producing limited-edition and specially commissioned vehicles, making a virtual-exclusive design an unconventional but on-brand move. This type of in-game branded content has become standard practice in modern AAA gaming titles.

title: Infiniti Delays Sporty QX80 SUV to Inject Even More Performance
summary: The Red Sport was supposed to arrive this year, but now the hopped-up QX80 is being pushed back to 2028 as Infiniti extracts even more performance.
whatHappening: Infiniti is delaying its high-performance QX80 Red Sport SUV from its originally planned 2025 launch to 2028. The luxury automaker is using the extra time to enhance the vehicle's performance capabilities beyond its initial specifications.
whoInvolved: Infiniti, Nissan's luxury vehicle division, is behind the delay. The company has been working to establish itself as a competitor in the high-performance luxury SUV segment against brands like BMW and Mercedes-Benz.
whyMatters: The delay signals Infiniti's commitment to delivering a genuinely competitive performance vehicle rather than rushing a product to market. For consumers interested in sporty luxury SUVs, this pushes their availability timeline back three years but potentially delivers a more capable competitor to established rivals.
moreContext: The QX80 Red Sport was conceived as Infiniti's answer to other luxury performance SUVs in an increasingly crowded segment. The decision to delay reflects broader industry trends where manufacturers are investing heavily in performance tuning to differentiate their vehicles. Infiniti has faced challenges establishing itself as a performance-focused luxury brand and has been restructuring its lineup in recent years. This extended development timeline suggests the company believes additional engineering refinement is necessary to meet performance targets and justify the Red Sport's premium positioning.

title: 2026 Hyundai Ioniq 5 N's Price Cut Makes 641 HP Way More Affordable
summary: With the arrival of the Ioniq 6 N sedan still up in the air, Hyundai's OG performance EV is more enticing than it's ever been.
whatHappening: Hyundai has cut the price on the 2026 Ioniq 5 N, its high-performance electric vehicle that delivers 641 horsepower. The price reduction makes this performance EV significantly more competitive in the market.
whoInvolved: Hyundai, the South Korean automaker, is making the move with its performance-focused N division. The company is positioning the Ioniq 5 N as a flagship performance EV while the status of the upcoming Ioniq 6 N sedan remains uncertain.
whyMatters: The price cut removes a major barrier for consumers interested in high-powered electric vehicles, potentially driving sales and market share in the growing performance EV segment. This move could establish the Ioniq 5 N as the go-to performance EV option as competitors continue developing their own offerings.
moreContext: The Ioniq 5 N represents Hyundai's push into performance electrification, competing against vehicles like the BMW i4 M and Tesla Model Y Performance. With 641 horsepower and performance credentials built for track use, the vehicle targets enthusiasts willing to pay premium prices for EVs. The Ioniq 6 N sedan, which would expand Hyundai's N-badged EV lineup, has faced delays and uncertainty around its development and release timeline. This price cut suggests Hyundai is doubling down on the Ioniq 5 N as its primary performance EV offering while the sedan's future remains in question.

title: California DMV Demands Drivers Retake Test or Lose Their License
summary: The government agency says there's a possibility that flagged patterns show evidence of cheating.
whatHappening: California's Department of Motor Vehicles is ordering certain drivers to retake their licensing exams or face license revocation. The DMV says it detected suspicious patterns suggesting widespread cheating during test administration.
whoInvolved: The California DMV, the state agency responsible for issuing and managing driver's licenses, is initiating the crackdown. Affected drivers across the state are being notified of the requirement to retest.
whyMatters: This could result in thousands of drivers losing legal driving privileges if they don't comply with retesting. The move signals the DMV's effort to ensure road safety by validating that license holders actually meet competency standards, potentially preventing accidents caused by unqualified drivers.
moreContext: Driver's license testing has faced integrity challenges in various states, with reports of cheating rings and compromised testing facilities in recent years. California's DMV oversees millions of licenses and has faced criticism over testing center operations and oversight. The specific flagged patterns suggest either systematic cheating at particular testing locations or coordinated fraud schemes. This enforcement action represents an unusual step by a state DMV to retroactively validate licenses already issued.

title: Nissan Is Preparing to Scrap Altima Sedan as It Pares U.S. Lineup
summary: Nissan's sedan lineup is about to be a party of one, according to the brand's chief product officer in North America.
whatHappening: Nissan is discontinuing the Altima sedan, leaving only the Maxima in its U.S. sedan lineup. The automaker is streamlining its North American vehicle portfolio as consumer preferences shift away from traditional sedans.
whoInvolved: Nissan, a major Japanese automaker, is making this strategic decision through its North American leadership, specifically guidance from the brand's chief product officer. The move reflects broader industry consolidation among legacy automakers.
whyMatters: This signals Nissan's pivot toward SUVs and crossovers where market demand and profit margins are stronger. For sedan buyers, it reduces affordable midsize options in a shrinking segment, while for Nissan it's a calculated bet that trucks and SUVs represent its growth future.
moreContext: The Altima has been a cornerstone of Nissan's lineup for decades, but sedan sales have plummeted industrywide as consumers increasingly favor SUVs and crossovers. This mirrors decisions by competitors like Honda and Toyota, who have already culled sedan lineups. Nissan's move to prioritize SUVs and crossovers aligns with broader market trends where these vehicle segments now dominate U.S. sales, with sedans representing less than 25% of the market compared to over 50% two decades ago.

title: Goodwood Festival of Speed Blends Competition, Exhibition, and Exhilaration
summary: What started as a one-day race up a driveway is now one of the biggest events on the calendar for hypercar reveals and motorsport celebrity appearances.
whatHappening: The Goodwood Festival of Speed is one of the automotive calendar's premier events, blending high-speed competition with exclusive hypercar debuts and star-studded motorsport appearances. The multi-day spectacle transforms a British estate into a showcase for cutting-edge performance vehicles and racing talent.
whoInvolved: Goodwood, a historic English estate, hosts the festival as a platform for luxury automakers, hypercar manufacturers, and professional racing drivers to display their latest innovations and achievements.
whyMatters: The festival has become the definitive venue for hypercar manufacturers to reveal next-generation vehicles to global media and enthusiasts, influencing industry narratives and consumer interest. Its growth signals the automotive industry's continued investment in high-performance experiences and exclusive events as marketing channels.
moreContext: The festival originated as a modest one-day race held on the Goodwood estate's private driveway, establishing its unique venue and intimate-yet-grand character. Over decades, it evolved into a multi-day international event attracting hundreds of thousands of attendees and becoming a mandatory calendar fixture for luxury brands launching flagship models. The event reflects broader automotive trends toward experiential marketing and the sustained cultural cachet of motorsport in brand positioning. Goodwood's combination of competitive racing, static exhibitions, and exclusive debuts has created a model that competitors struggle to replicate, cementing its status as the premier automotive gathering outside Formula 1 and traditional motorsport championships.

title: View Photos of the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed
summary: See photos of the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is releasing a photo gallery showcasing the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed. The collection captures vehicles and action from multiple angles at the prestigious automotive event.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is documenting the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed—an annual showcase held at Goodwood House in West Sussex, England that attracts manufacturers, collectors, and racing teams from around the world.
whyMatters: The photo coverage provides enthusiasts with visual access to rare vehicles, concept cars, and performance machines that appear at one of the automotive world's most exclusive events. This documentation helps automotive fans and industry observers stay informed about new reveals and classic car presentations without attending in person.
moreContext: The Goodwood Festival of Speed, held annually since 1993, is one of the world's largest automotive events, known for its hillclimb races and exclusive vehicle debuts. The event attracts manufacturers showcasing everything from hypercars to restored classics, and it serves as a major platform for automotive announcements. The 2026 edition continues this tradition of bringing together the automotive industry's most significant players and most coveted vehicles in one location.

title: Red Bull’s $7.5 Million RB17 Breaks Cover at the Goodwood Festival of Speed
summary: Red Bull brought a running prototype to the legendary British festival.
whatHappening: Red Bull has unveiled its $7.5 million RB17 hypercar at the Goodwood Festival of Speed, one of the automotive world's most prestigious events. The company brought a running prototype to the legendary British festival, marking a significant public debut for the exclusive vehicle.
whoInvolved: Red Bull, the energy drink company and Formula 1 powerhouse, partnered with legendary F1 designer Adrian Newey to create the RB17. The vehicle represents Red Bull's expansion into limited-production hypercars, leveraging the company's motorsport expertise and engineering prowess.
whyMatters: The RB17's debut signals Red Bull's serious ambitions beyond F1 into the ultra-luxury hypercar market, potentially establishing a new revenue stream and brand extension. The appearance at Goodwood—where automotive enthusiasts and industry insiders gather—amplifies visibility for the exclusive vehicle and validates Red Bull's credentials as a legitimate manufacturer beyond beverages and racing.
moreContext: Red Bull has been leveraging its F1 success and technical expertise to diversify into high-performance vehicles, with the RB17 representing the company's flagship automotive project. Goodwood Festival of Speed, held annually in West Sussex, England, is where manufacturers traditionally showcase their most innovative and exclusive vehicles to a global audience of collectors and enthusiasts. The $7.5 million price point positions the RB17 in the elite hypercar segment alongside vehicles from McLaren, Ferrari, and Pagani. By bringing a running prototype rather than just a concept, Red Bull demonstrated confidence in the vehicle's development and readiness for production.

title: 'The Grand Tour' Will Return on September 4 with a New Trio of Hosts
summary: YouTube's Throttle House duo and TikTok's Francis Bourgeois will officially take on the legendary show come September.
whatHappening: The Grand Tour, the iconic automotive show, is making a comeback on September 4 with an entirely new hosting lineup. YouTube's Throttle House duo and TikTok personality Francis Bourgeois will take over the legendary series.
whoInvolved: Throttle House, a popular YouTube automotive channel known for entertaining car reviews, and Francis Bourgeois, a TikTok creator famous for train and vehicle enthusiasm content, are stepping into hosting roles for the revived show. YouTube is producing the new iteration of the series.
whyMatters: This marks a significant shift in automotive entertainment, bringing fresh digital-native talent to a traditionally established show format. The move signals how legacy media properties are adapting by recruiting creators with existing younger audiences and social media expertise. This could attract a broader, younger demographic to automotive content while setting a precedent for how classic TV franchises are being reimagined for streaming platforms.
moreContext: The Grand Tour originally launched in 2016 as Amazon Prime Video's answer to Top Gear, starring Jeremy Clarkson, James May, and Richard Hammond. The previous iteration ran for multiple seasons before going on hiatus. By tapping into creators with strong social media followings—Throttle House has built a massive YouTube audience for accessible car content, while Francis Bourgeois has millions of TikTok followers—the show is positioning itself to compete in the streaming landscape where creator-led content dominates audience engagement.

title: 2027 Ram 1500's First Roush Truck Is a V-8 Alternative to the RHO
summary: The Direct Connection by Roush package adds off-road capability, visual flair, and sweet Hemi noise.
whatHappening: Roush is introducing its first performance package for the 2027 Ram 1500, called Direct Connection by Roush, which delivers V-8 power and enhanced off-road capabilities. The package positions itself as a V-8 alternative to Ram's new RHO model, complete with visual upgrades and distinctive engine sound.
whoInvolved: Roush, the legendary performance and specialty vehicle manufacturer, is partnering with Ram (a division of Stellantis) to create this aftermarket package. Roush has built its reputation over decades customizing Ford vehicles and developing high-performance packages across multiple truck platforms.
whyMatters: This marks Roush's entry into the Ram truck customization market during a critical industry shift toward electrification and hybrid powertrains. The V-8 offering appeals to traditional truck buyers seeking conventional engine performance while Ram itself pivots toward its new RHO variant, potentially capturing customers who want Hemi power alongside modern off-road technology.
moreContext: The Ram 1500 has long been a performance-truck favorite, and the 2027 generation represents a significant redesign incorporating electrification options like the new RHO hybrid variant. Roush's Direct Connection brand focuses on maintaining gas-powered alternatives as manufacturers increasingly invest in hybrid and electric vehicles. This partnership reflects the market reality that substantial truck buyer segments remain committed to traditional V-8 engines, giving specialty manufacturers like Roush continued opportunity. The package combines Roush's customization expertise with Ram's truck platform, likely featuring suspension upgrades, styling enhancements, and powertrain refinements.

title: VW Group Reportedly Targeting Jetta, Porsche Models as Cut Candidates
summary: With the conglomerate planning to axe up to half of its global lineup, a German newspaper claims to know the hit list.
whatHappening: Volkswagen Group is reportedly planning to eliminate up to half of its global vehicle lineup as part of a major restructuring. A German newspaper claims to have identified specific models targeted for discontinuation, including the Jetta and certain Porsche models.
whoInvolved: Volkswagen Group, the world's largest automaker by volume, which owns multiple brands including VW, Audi, Porsche, Skoda, and others. The report originates from German media sources covering the conglomerate's strategic decisions.
whyMatters: This represents a massive portfolio contraction that could reshape the automotive market and signal how legacy automakers are responding to the EV transition and slowing global demand. The cuts would affect millions of potential customers and reshape competition in key segments like mainstream sedans and luxury sports cars.
moreContext: VW Group has been under pressure to streamline operations and reduce costs while investing heavily in electric vehicle development. The Jetta, a popular budget sedan particularly in emerging markets, and various Porsche models represent different market segments the group is willing to sacrifice. This restructuring reflects broader industry trends where traditional automakers are consolidating portfolios to focus resources on electrification and autonomous driving technologies. The move comes as VW battles profitability challenges and competition from both established rivals and new EV-focused manufacturers.

title: Californians Buying Their First EV Can Save $3500 with New Incentive
summary: While there's a $50,000 price cap, that doesn't apply to California-based automakers like Rivian and Lucid Motors.
whatHappening: California is offering a new $3,500 incentive for first-time EV buyers. The program comes with a $50,000 price cap on vehicles, though California-based automakers like Rivian and Lucid Motors are exempt from this limit.
whoInvolved: California state government is administering the incentive program. Rivian and Lucid Motors, both California-based EV manufacturers, benefit from the exemption that allows their higher-priced vehicles to qualify.
whyMatters: The incentive lowers the barrier to EV adoption for first-time buyers in the nation's largest car market, potentially accelerating electric vehicle sales. The exemption for California automakers gives Rivian and Lucid a competitive advantage by allowing luxury models to remain eligible despite exceeding the $50,000 cap.
moreContext: EV incentives have become a key policy tool as states and the federal government work to meet climate goals and boost electric vehicle adoption. California has historically led the U.S. in EV sales and policy innovation. The $50,000 price cap targets mass-market vehicles while the carve-out for homegrown manufacturers aims to support the state's burgeoning EV industry. This move reflects tensions between democratizing EV access and supporting local companies.

title: View Exterior Photos of the 2026 Mercedes-Benz CLA250+
summary: See the exterior of the 2026 Mercedes-Benz CLA250+ from every angle.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz is unveiling exterior photos of its 2026 CLA250+ model, offering a comprehensive visual preview from multiple angles. The updated sedan represents the German automaker's latest iteration in the affordable luxury compact segment.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is showcasing the 2026 CLA250+. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is providing the photo gallery and coverage of the vehicle's exterior design.
whyMatters: The 2026 CLA250+ refresh is significant for consumers considering entry-level luxury sedans, as it reflects current design trends and competitive positioning in the crowded compact luxury market. The exterior reveal helps potential buyers assess styling changes and whether the model meets their aesthetic and practical needs before official launch.
moreContext: The Mercedes-Benz CLA has been a key volume driver for the brand since its 2013 introduction, targeting younger, design-conscious buyers seeking luxury at lower price points. The CLA line has expanded to include multiple variants and powertrains over the years. For 2026, manufacturers are increasingly incorporating bold exterior redesigns to justify model-year updates amid competitive pressure from BMW, Audi, and Lexus in the entry-luxury segment. Visual previews like this help build anticipation and social media engagement ahead of official specifications and pricing announcements.

title: Acura's Integra 40 Racer Is a Celebration of Japanese-American Motorsport History
summary: We recruit our in-house Integra superfan to translate the experience of driving a "new" 40-year-old race car.
whatHappening: Acura has restored and is showcasing a historic Integra 40 race car, tapping an in-house enthusiast to document the driving experience of this four-decade-old machine. The project celebrates the intersection of Japanese engineering prowess and American motorsport culture.
whoInvolved: Acura, Honda's luxury division, is behind the restoration effort, leveraging its deep roots in performance engineering and motorsport heritage. Car and Driver, a major automotive publication, is covering the story through the perspective of a staff member with particular passion for the Integra nameplate.
whyMatters: The story underscores the enduring appeal of classic Japanese sports cars and Acura's efforts to connect modern audiences with its performance legacy. This type of heritage celebration can strengthen brand loyalty and demonstrate the lasting engineering quality of vehicles from that era.
moreContext: The Integra nameplate has held iconic status in Japanese-American car culture since its 1986 debut, particularly among tuning and racing communities. The Integra 40 designation likely references a significant racing variant or anniversary model from the car's history. By restoring and driving a period race car rather than simply displaying it statically, Acura is tapping into the growing trend of experiential marketing and nostalgia-driven content that resonates with automotive enthusiasts. This aligns with broader industry interest in preserving and celebrating automotive heritage during a period of rapid electrification.

title: MG's GO! Concept Is a Funky Electric Mini-Rivaling Hot Hatchback
summary: MG hasn't had a presence in the United States for almost 50 years, but this little hatch makes us wish it still did.
whatHappening: MG has unveiled the GO!, a funky electric mini hot hatchback concept that's generating buzz among automotive enthusiasts. The vehicle combines compact dimensions with performance and electric powertrain appeal in a retro-inspired package.
whoInvolved: MG, the British-heritage automotive brand now owned by Chinese conglomerate SAIC, is behind this concept vehicle. The company last sold cars in the U.S. market nearly 50 years ago but has been reviving its global presence through new models and electric vehicle development.
whyMatters: The GO! concept signals MG's potential interest in re-entering the highly competitive American market with an electric vehicle that could rival established players like Mini Cooper and hot hatchback makers. A successful U.S. return could shake up the affordable EV segment and give consumers more quirky, performance-oriented options in the under-$30,000 range.
moreContext: MG exited the U.S. market around 1980 but has experienced a global resurgence in recent years, particularly in Europe and Asia, where it's become one of the world's best-selling EV brands. The electric hatchback segment is increasingly crowded with offerings from established automakers like Volkswagen, Hyundai, and Kia, all competing for younger, design-conscious buyers. Mini Coopers and hot hatchbacks have maintained a dedicated enthusiast following in America for decades. A concept vehicle like the GO! typically signals MG's design direction and market testing before potential production decisions.

title: View Photos of the 1987 Acura Integra 40 Racer
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 1987 Acura Integra 40 Racer from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is showcasing the 1987 Acura Integra 40 Racer with a comprehensive photo gallery capturing both interior and exterior details. Readers can examine the classic race car from multiple angles.
whoInvolved: Acura, Honda's luxury division, developed the Integra 40 Racer as a competition-focused variant of their iconic Integra model. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is documenting the vehicle's design and specifications.
whyMatters: This coverage highlights an important chapter in Acura's motorsports heritage and the early performance credentials of the Integra nameplate. The 1987 model represents a pivotal moment when Japanese manufacturers were gaining credibility in racing and performance segments traditionally dominated by American and European brands.
moreContext: The Acura Integra debuted in 1986 as a compact sport sedan and quickly became a favorite among enthusiasts and racers. The 40 Racer designation reflected Acura's racing ambitions during the late 1980s, a period when Japanese automakers were aggressively pursuing motorsports competition. The Integra would eventually become one of the most celebrated sport compacts of its generation, spawning multiple generations and earning a dedicated tuning community. Photo galleries like this serve to preserve automotive history and appeal to enthusiasts interested in vintage performance vehicles.

title: 1954 Studebaker Race Car For Sale on Bring a Trailer
summary: This road-race veteran has NASCAR underpinnings, yet it's still street-legal.
whatHappening: A rare 1954 Studebaker race car is being auctioned on Bring a Trailer, combining competitive racing heritage with street-legal registration. The vehicle features NASCAR-derived performance components beneath its classic Studebaker body.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer, the online collector car auction platform, is facilitating the sale. Studebaker, the now-defunct American automaker, originally built this vehicle during the 1950s when the company was still producing cars.
whyMatters: The sale highlights collector interest in early 1950s American racing history and dual-purpose vehicles that blur the line between street and track. Such vehicles represent an era when amateur and professional racing was more accessible and less regulated than modern motorsports.
moreContext: The 1950s marked a golden age for American automotive racing, with manufacturers like Studebaker competing in various racing series. Studebaker, founded in 1852 as a wagon maker before transitioning to automobiles, was known for producing nimble, efficient vehicles that performed well in racing despite the company's smaller scale compared to Big Three competitors. The rarity of surviving Studebaker race cars from this period makes authentic examples increasingly valuable among collectors. The dual street-legal and racing credentials of this particular car exemplify how automotive regulations and racing rules were more permissive during the 1950s.

title: 2001 Lexus SC430 vs. Mercedes-Benz CLK430 Comparison Test
summary: From the archive: Fancy convertibles of the Y2K era.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is revisiting a classic comparison test between two luxury convertibles from the early 2000s: the Lexus SC430 and Mercedes-Benz CLK430. This archive piece evaluates how these premium vehicles stacked up against each other during the Y2K era.
whoInvolved: Lexus, Toyota's luxury division, and Mercedes-Benz, the German automotive luxury brand, both competed in the high-end convertible market. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, conducted the original testing and analysis of these vehicles.
whyMatters: These vehicles represent a defining moment in luxury convertible design when manufacturers were balancing performance, comfort, and cutting-edge technology for affluent buyers. Retrospective comparisons like this offer insight into how automotive engineering and consumer preferences have evolved over two decades.
moreContext: The early 2000s marked a golden age for luxury convertibles, as manufacturers invested heavily in this segment before market shifts toward SUVs and crossovers. The Lexus SC430, known for reliability and understated elegance, competed directly with Mercedes' CLK-Class convertible, which emphasized sporty handling and performance. Both vehicles featured retractable hard-top technology, a premium feature at the time. Archive content like this serves automotive enthusiasts and historians by documenting how different manufacturers approached luxury convertible design philosophy during this era.

title: The Bestselling Luxury Vehicles of 2026
summary: Which models from the likes of BMW, Mercedes, and Lexus are the biggest hitters on the sales charts? We've gathered the numbers and ranked the top 10 sellers.
whatHappening: Car and Driver has released its rankings of the top 10 best-selling luxury vehicles in 2026. The analysis reveals which models from premium automakers are dominating consumer preferences and sales charts.
whoInvolved: Major luxury automotive brands BMW, Mercedes-Benz, and Lexus are the primary players featured in this sales analysis. These three manufacturers represent some of the world's most established and competitive segments in the premium vehicle market.
whyMatters: Understanding which luxury models lead in sales provides insight into consumer spending patterns and preferences during an economic period. The rankings signal which automakers are successfully capturing high-margin vehicle sales and maintaining market dominance in the lucrative luxury segment.
moreContext: The luxury vehicle market has undergone significant shifts in recent years with the rise of luxury SUVs and crossovers overshadowing traditional sedans. Electric and hybrid powertrains have increasingly become standard offerings across luxury lineups as manufacturers respond to environmental concerns and changing buyer preferences. 2026 represents a critical year as the automotive industry navigates the transition toward electrification while maintaining brand prestige. Sales data from this period reflects consumer appetite for premium features, performance, and sustainability in high-end vehicles.

title: View Photos of the 2001 Lexus SC430 vs. Mercedes-Benz CLK430
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 2001 Lexus SC430 and Mercedes-Benz CLK430 from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is publishing a visual comparison of two luxury convertibles from the early 2000s: the 2001 Lexus SC430 and Mercedes-Benz CLK430. Readers can view both vehicles from multiple angles to compare their interior and exterior design.
whoInvolved: Lexus, Toyota's luxury division, and Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, both competed in the premium convertible segment during the early 2000s. Car and Driver is a leading automotive publication known for comprehensive vehicle comparisons and photography.
whyMatters: These comparisons help enthusiasts and potential buyers understand the design philosophies and practical differences between competing luxury vehicles. For classic car collectors and automotive historians, visual documentation of these models preserves important styling details from a significant era in convertible design.
moreContext: The 2001 model year represents the first generation SC430, which debuted as Lexus's answer to the Mercedes-Benz SL-Class and CLK-Class convertibles. The CLK430 was Mercedes' mid-range luxury convertible offering, positioned below the iconic SL-Class. Both vehicles featured retractable hardtops, a cutting-edge technology for convertibles at the time, and competed for affluent buyers seeking open-air luxury. The early 2000s marked a golden era for premium convertibles before market shifts toward SUVs and crossovers began reshaping luxury automakers' lineups.

title: This 2012 Cadillac CTS-V Wagon on Bring a Trailer Has a Little Extra
summary: A wider stance and more horsepower make this hot rod Caddy wagon not like the others.
whatHappening: A customized 2012 Cadillac CTS-V Wagon is being auctioned on Bring a Trailer with performance and styling upgrades that distinguish it from the standard model. The vehicle features a wider stance and increased horsepower compared to the factory version.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer, the online auction platform specializing in collectible and enthusiast vehicles, is hosting the sale. The car is a Cadillac CTS-V Wagon, General Motors' high-performance take on their mid-size sedan platform.
whyMatters: Customized performance vehicles command premium prices on specialty auction platforms, reflecting growing collector interest in modified American muscle cars and wagons. This sale demonstrates the market's appetite for unique automotive builds that combine practicality with serious performance upgrades.
moreContext: The Cadillac CTS-V Wagon was already a rare offering—GM produced it for only two generations (2009-2014) before discontinuing it, making it a niche collector vehicle. Wagon enthusiasts have increasingly turned to modified versions of performance models to combine cargo versatility with track-capable power. Bring a Trailer has become the premier marketplace for such builds, regularly attracting six-figure bids for distinctive custom vehicles. The CTS-V platform, powered by a 6.2L supercharged V8, provides an excellent foundation for modifications that can significantly exceed its 556 factory horsepower.

title: Bizzarrini Brings a 1960s Beauty to Life with Modern Engineering
summary: Only 10 of these gorgeous targa-topped throwbacks will be built.
whatHappening: Bizzarrini is reviving a classic 1960s sports car design with modern engineering and will produce only 10 examples of this targa-topped vehicle. The limited production run combines vintage aesthetics with contemporary performance and technology.
whoInvolved: Bizzarrini is an Italian automaker known for creating high-performance sports cars, particularly the iconic 5300 GT from the 1960s. The company has returned to manufacturing after decades away from production.
whyMatters: This project taps into the growing luxury market for low-volume, hand-crafted vehicles that blend retro design with modern engineering. The extremely limited production (10 units) ensures exclusivity and collector appeal while positioning Bizzarrini as a niche player in the boutique automaker space.
moreContext: The original Bizzarrini 5300 GT was a race-winning masterpiece from the 1960s that competed against Ferraris and other European supercars. The resurgence of retro-inspired sports cars has gained momentum in recent years, with brands like Nissan Z and Toyota GR86 drawing on heritage design language. Bizzarrini's return to production capitalizes on both nostalgia for classic Italian design and wealthy collectors' appetite for limited-edition vehicles with authentic provenance. The targa-top configuration offers an open-air driving experience while maintaining structural rigidity and modern safety standards.

title: Could the Currently Banned Polestar 3 Survive as a Volvo?
summary: Built in South Carolina, the Polestar 3 shares its key hardware and software with the Volvo EX90.
whatHappening: The Polestar 3, currently banned from the U.S. market, could potentially continue sales under the Volvo brand name. The vehicles share identical core hardware and software, suggesting a rebrand could bypass current restrictions.
whoInvolved: Polestar is Volvo's performance-focused EV subbrand, while Volvo manufactures the Polestar 3 at its South Carolina facility. Both companies are owned by Chinese conglomerate Geely-Volvo.
whyMatters: A successful rebrand could allow Volvo to maintain U.S. EV sales and production at its South Carolina plant while circumventing regulatory barriers. This approach would preserve jobs and revenue streams threatened by the Polestar 3's ban, while testing whether corporate restructuring can overcome trade or regulatory obstacles.
moreContext: The Polestar 3 faces U.S. market restrictions due to regulatory or trade-related issues stemming from its Chinese ownership structure. The vehicle shares technical platforms with the Volvo EX90, another premium EV from the same corporate family. Volvo has a long-established U.S. presence and manufacturing operations, potentially giving it different regulatory standing than the newer Polestar brand. This situation reflects broader tensions between Chinese automotive investment in the U.S. and government scrutiny of foreign ownership in the EV sector.
